# Architecture

**Analysis Date:** 2025-07-15

## Pattern Overview

**Overall:** Wails v3 desktop app ŌĆö Go backend exposing services via RPC bindings, Vue 3 + TypeScript SPA frontend consuming them through auto-generated TypeScript clients.

**Key Characteristics:**
- **Service-oriented backend:** Go structs with exported methods become frontend-callable RPC functions via Wails binding generation
- **Single-process monolith:** Go backend + embedded frontend assets run in one OS process with a native webview
- **Domain-driven types:** Shared `domain` package defines canonical Go structs; Wails generates corresponding TypeScript classes in `frontend/bindings/`
- **Local-first storage:** SQLite database with WAL mode, stored in OS-appropriate data directory
- **Mock-first frontend:** Pinia stores include inline mock data and a `useMock` flag, enabling frontend development without the Go backend running

## Layers

**Domain Layer:**
- Purpose: Canonical type definitions shared across Go packages
- Location: `domain/types.go`
- Contains: `User`, `Project`, `Task`, `TaskADOLink`, `TaskDep` structs with JSON tags
- Depends on: Nothing (leaf package)
- Used by: `internal/app`, `internal/auth`, generated bindings

**ADO Domain Layer:**
- Purpose: Azure DevOps-specific type definitions (PRs, work items)
- Location: `pkg/ado/domain/types.go`
- Contains: `ADOPullRequest`, `ADOWorkItem` structs and status/type constants
- Depends on: Nothing (leaf package)
- Used by: Future ADO integration services; DB schema already includes corresponding tables

**Database Layer:**
- Purpose: SQLite connection management, schema migration, thin wrapper around `database/sql`
- Location: `internal/db/db.go`
- Contains: `DB` struct (wraps `*sql.DB`), `Open()`, `migrate()`, embedded schema DDL
- Depends on: `github.com/mattn/go-sqlite3`
- Used by: All service constructors in `internal/app` and `internal/auth`

**Service Layer (Backend):**
- Purpose: Business logic exposed to frontend via Wails bindings
- Location: `internal/app/` (tasks, projects, deps), `internal/auth/auth.go`, `internal/config/service.go`
- Contains: Service structs with exported methods that Wails auto-generates TypeScript bindings for
- Depends on: `internal/db`, `domain`
- Used by: `main.go` (registration), frontend via generated bindings

  Services registered in `main.go`:
  - `config.ConfigService` ŌĆö read/write app config via Viper (`internal/config/service.go`)
  - `app.TaskService` ŌĆö full CRUD + filtering, subtasks, tags (`internal/app/tasks.go`)
  - `app.ProjectService` ŌĆö project CRUD (`internal/app/projects.go`)
  - `app.DependencyService` ŌĆö task deps with cycle detection (`internal/app/deps.go`)
  - `auth.AuthService` ŌĆö OAuth2 PKCE + PAT auth with Microsoft Entra ID (`internal/auth/auth.go`)

**Configuration Layer:**
- Purpose: App config initialization, defaults, OS-specific paths
- Location: `internal/config/config.go` (init + accessors), `internal/config/service.go` (Wails-bound service)
- Contains: Viper-based config with YAML file persistence, XBT_ env prefix, convenience getters
- Depends on: `github.com/spf13/viper`
- Used by: `main.go`, `ConfigService`, any code needing config values

**Generated Bindings Layer:**
- Purpose: Auto-generated TypeScript clients that bridge frontend ŌåÆ backend RPC calls
- Location: `frontend/bindings/`
- Contains: TypeScript functions calling `$Call.ByID(hashID, ...args)` for each Go service method, plus model classes with `createFrom()` factory methods
- Depends on: `@wailsio/runtime`
- Used by: Pinia stores via dynamic `import()` (lazy-loaded)
- **Generated:** Yes ŌĆö DO NOT EDIT. Regenerated by `wails3 dev` or `wails3 generate bindings`

  Key binding files:
  - `frontend/bindings/dev.azure.com/xbox/xb-tasks/internal/app/taskservice.ts`
  - `frontend/bindings/dev.azure.com/xbox/xb-tasks/internal/app/projectservice.ts`
  - `frontend/bindings/dev.azure.com/xbox/xb-tasks/internal/app/dependencyservice.ts`
  - `frontend/bindings/dev.azure.com/xbox/xb-tasks/internal/config/configservice.ts`
  - `frontend/bindings/dev.azure.com/xbox/xb-tasks/internal/auth/authservice.ts`
  - `frontend/bindings/dev.azure.com/xbox/xb-tasks/domain/models.ts` (TS model classes)

**State Management Layer (Frontend):**
- Purpose: Reactive state, data fetching, mock fallback logic
- Location: `frontend/src/stores/`
- Contains: Pinia stores using Composition API (`defineStore` with `setup()` syntax)
- Depends on: Generated bindings (lazy-imported), Vue reactivity
- Used by: Views and components

  Stores:
  - `frontend/src/stores/tasks.ts` ŌĆö task state, CRUD, filtering, grouping, stats, mock data
  - `frontend/src/stores/projects.ts` ŌĆö project state, CRUD, mock data
  - `frontend/src/stores/auth.ts` ŌĆö user state, sign in/out, session restore

**View Layer (Frontend):**
- Purpose: Full-page route components
- Location: `frontend/src/views/`
- Contains: Vue SFCs ŌĆö one per route
- Depends on: Stores, components, composables
- Used by: Vue Router

**Component Layer (Frontend):**
- Purpose: Reusable UI pieces and layout shells
- Location: `frontend/src/components/` (app components), `frontend/src/components/ui/` (primitives)
- Contains: App-specific components (Sidebar, TaskDetail, TaskRow, CommandPalette, PageHeader) + shadcn-vue style UI primitives (Badge, Button, Card, Dialog, etc.)
- Depends on: Stores, lib/utils, lucide-vue-next icons
- Used by: Views, layouts

**Layout Layer (Frontend):**
- Purpose: Page shell with sidebar, top bar, activity panel
- Location: `frontend/src/layouts/AppShell.vue`
- Contains: Single layout component wrapping all non-login routes
- Depends on: Sidebar, CommandPalette, stores
- Used by: `App.vue` (conditionally via `route.meta.hideShell`)

## Data Flow

**Task CRUD (Frontend ŌåÆ Backend ŌåÆ DB):**

1. User interacts in `TasksView.vue` (e.g., creates task via inline input)
2. View calls `taskStore.createTask(title, priority)`
3. Store checks `useMock` flag ŌĆö if true, creates mock task in-memory and returns
4. If not mock: dynamically imports `../../bindings/.../taskservice` and calls `Create()`
5. Binding calls `$Call.ByID(hashID, ...)` ŌĆö Wails runtime sends IPC message to Go process
6. Go `TaskService.Create()` executes SQL INSERT via `s.db.Exec()`
7. Go method returns `domain.Task` ŌĆö Wails serializes to JSON, sends back to frontend
8. Binding deserializes via `domain$0.Task.createFrom()`, store updates reactive `tasks` ref

**Authentication Flow (OAuth2 PKCE):**

1. User clicks "Sign in with Microsoft" on `LoginView.vue`
2. `authStore.signIn()` calls `AuthService.SignIn()` via binding
3. Go `AuthService.SignIn()`:
   a. Generates PKCE code verifier + challenge
   b. Starts ephemeral HTTP server on random localhost port
   c. Opens browser to Microsoft Entra ID authorize URL
   d. Waits for callback with auth code (5-minute timeout)
   e. Exchanges code for tokens
   f. Saves tokens to OS keyring via `go-keyring`
   g. Fetches user profile from Microsoft Graph API
   h. Upserts user in SQLite
   i. Emits `auth:state-changed` Wails event
4. Returns `*domain.User` to frontend

**Session Restore (Startup):**

1. `main.go` launches `go authService.TryRestoreSession()` in background goroutine
2. Attempts to load refresh token from OS keyring
3. If found, refreshes via OAuth2 token source, saves new tokens
4. If not found, checks for PAT in keyring
5. On success, sets `currentUser` ŌĆö frontend can call `GetCurrentUser()` to pick it up

**State Management:**
- **Backend state:** SQLite database (persistent), in-memory `currentUser` on `AuthService`
- **Frontend state:** Pinia stores with reactive refs. Each store maintains its own loading/error state.
- **Mock mode:** Stores default to `useMock = true` with hardcoded test data. On first binding call failure, `useMock` is set to `true` as fallback. This enables standalone frontend development.
- **Cross-process events:** `AuthService` emits Wails events (`auth:state-changed`) that the frontend can subscribe to via `@wailsio/runtime`

## Key Abstractions

**Wails Service Pattern:**
- Purpose: Bridge Go methods to frontend-callable RPC
- Examples: `internal/app/tasks.go` (`TaskService`), `internal/auth/auth.go` (`AuthService`)
- Pattern: Define Go struct with exported methods ŌåÆ register with `application.NewService(instance)` ŌåÆ Wails generates TypeScript bindings. Method signatures directly become the frontend API.

**DB Wrapper:**
- Purpose: Thin SQLite wrapper with auto-migration
- Examples: `internal/db/db.go`
- Pattern: Embeds `*sql.DB`, adds `Open()` with WAL mode and `migrate()` with embedded DDL string. Services receive `*db.DB` via constructor injection.

**Pinia Store with Mock Fallback:**
- Purpose: Frontend data layer with offline/development support
- Examples: `frontend/src/stores/tasks.ts`, `frontend/src/stores/projects.ts`
- Pattern: Each store has a module-level `useMock` boolean and `MOCK_*` arrays. All data methods check `useMock` first. If binding import fails (no Go backend), catch block sets `useMock = true` and falls back.

**shadcn-vue UI Primitives:**
- Purpose: Consistent, accessible low-level UI components
- Examples: `frontend/src/components/ui/button/Button.vue`, `frontend/src/components/ui/card/Card.vue`
- Pattern: Each primitive in its own directory with `index.ts` barrel export. Built on `reka-ui` (headless) + `class-variance-authority` for variant styling + Tailwind CSS.

**Custom Domain UI Components:**
- Purpose: App-specific visual elements combining primitives
- Examples: `frontend/src/components/ui/StatusBadge.vue`, `frontend/src/components/ui/PriorityBadge.vue`, `frontend/src/components/ui/AdoBadge.vue`, `frontend/src/components/ui/TagChip.vue`
- Pattern: Single-file components in `components/ui/` root (not in subdirectories). Accept domain props (status string, priority string, etc.) and render styled badges.

## Entry Points

**Go Application Entry:**
- Location: `main.go`
- Triggers: OS process launch
- Responsibilities:
  1. Initialize config via `config.Init()`
  2. Open SQLite database via `db.Open(config.DBPath())`
  3. Create service instances with DI (database injection)
  4. Create Wails app, register all services
  5. Set up system tray (show/hide/quit)
  6. Create main window with macOS-specific chrome (hidden title bar, translucent backdrop)
  7. Intercept window close to hide instead of quit (tray app pattern)
  8. Kick off background session restore
  9. Call `wailsApp.Run()`

**Frontend Entry:**
- Location: `frontend/src/main.ts`
- Triggers: Webview loads `/` URL
- Responsibilities: Create Vue app, install Pinia + Vue Router, mount to `#app`

**Vue App Root:**
- Location: `frontend/src/App.vue`
- Triggers: Vue mount
- Responsibilities: Initialize theme composable, conditionally wrap routes in `AppShell` layout (login route uses `meta.hideShell: true` to render without shell)

**Router:**
- Location: `frontend/src/router/index.ts`
- Uses `createWebHashHistory()` (hash-based routing for desktop webview)
- Default route `/` redirects to `/tasks`
- Routes: `/tasks`, `/dashboard`, `/projects`, `/settings`, `/login`, plus 4 playground routes (dev only)

## Error Handling

**Strategy:** Minimal ŌĆö errors bubble up as Go `error` returns or frontend catch blocks

**Go Backend Patterns:**
- Services return `(Type, error)` tuples with `fmt.Errorf("context: %w", err)` wrapping
- `main.go` uses `log.Fatalf` for fatal startup errors (config init, DB open)
- Row scan errors are logged with `log.Printf` and skipped (soft failure during list operations)
- Auth callback has 5-minute timeout with context cancellation

**Frontend Patterns:**
- Stores wrap binding calls in try/catch, falling back to mock mode on failure
- Auth store sets `error.value` string for UI display
- No global error boundary or toast notification system currently exists

## Cross-Cutting Concerns

**Logging:** `log.Printf`/`log.Fatalf` from Go stdlib. Config supports `log.level` setting but no structured logging framework is wired up yet.

**Validation:** Inline in service methods ŌĆö basic required field checks (e.g., `title == ""` returns error). No validation library. The SQLite schema also has CHECK constraints on `status` and `priority` columns.

**Authentication:** OAuth2 PKCE flow with Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD), plus PAT fallback for development. Tokens stored in OS keyring (`go-keyring`). User persisted in `users` SQLite table. Frontend auth store has mock fallback.

**Configuration:** Viper-based with layered sources: defaults ŌåÆ YAML config file ŌåÆ `XBT_` env vars. Config file auto-created on first run. OS-specific paths (macOS: `~/Library/Application Support/team-ado-tool/config.yaml`).

**Theming:** Dark/light mode via `@vueuse/core` `useColorMode` composable. Toggles `dark` CSS class on `<html>`. CSS variables in `frontend/src/style.css` define both light and dark palettes. shadcn-vue components use oklch color tokens.

**Window Management:** macOS-optimized with hidden inset title bar, translucent backdrop. Window close intercepted to hide (tray app). System tray with show/quit menu. Window dimensions configurable via settings.

---

*Architecture analysis: 2025-07-15*
